title: Print Numbers Skipping Multiples of a Divisor
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that reads N and D, then prints the integers from 1 to N inclusive, skipping any that are multiples of D.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 <= N <= 20)
- Line 2: integer D (2 <= D <= N)

## Output
Print the surviving integers separated by spaces on one line, followed by a newline. If no integers survive (all are multiples of D), print an empty line.

## Requirements
1. Use a `for` loop from 1 to N inclusive.
2. Use `continue` to skip values where `i % D == 0`.
3. Print each non-skipped value followed by a space.
4. Print a newline after all values.
5. Edge cases covered by hidden tests: D == 2 (skip all evens), a range where every number is a multiple of D.

test_cases.label: Sample: skip multiples of 3 up to 9
Hidden: single value that is a multiple

title: Fix the Bug: Find First Value Above Threshold
prompt_md:
## Goal
The program below is supposed to read N integers and print the first one that is greater than a threshold T, then stop. If no value exceeds T, print `None`. It contains exactly one logic bug — find and fix it.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 <= N <= 10), then integer T on the same line.
- Lines 2..N+1: one integer per line.

## Output
Print exactly one line: either `Found: V` where V is the first value greater than T, or `None` if no such value exists.

## Requirements
1. Use `break` to exit as soon as the first qualifying value is found.
2. After the loop, print `Found: V` if a qualifying value was found, or `None` otherwise.
3. Hidden tests cover: qualifying value in the first position, qualifying value in the last position, no qualifying value.

test_cases.label: Sample: first qualifying value at position 2
Hidden: qualifying value in first position
